title: feedback loop missing in logic cell.
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Yosys 0.9+932 (git sha1 fcce9401, clang 9.0.0 -fPIC -Os)

input : top.v

```
module top (
    output PIN_21, PIN_22, PIN_23, PIN_24, USBPU,
    input CLK, PIN_1, PIN_2, PIN_3, PIN_4, PIN_5, PIN_6, PIN_7, PIN_8, PIN_9, PIN_10, PIN_11, PIN_12, PIN_13
);

    reg compareBuf = 0;
    assign PIN_21 = compareBuf;

    always @(posedge CLK) begin
        compareBuf <= IN_1 < PIN_2 ? 1 : PIN_1 > PIN_2 ? 0 : compareBuf;
    end


endmodule

```

## Expected behavior

Have one LC with 3 inputs, the 2 from the pins and a third is a feedback loop.

## Actual behavior

There are 2 LCs:

1. has the LUT set to `PIN_1 < PIN_2` and has the dff enable.

2. has the LUT set to `PIN_1 ^ PIN_2` and is affecting the enable of the dff from the first LC.

So the expected and actual behavior have the same outcome, but is there a way to tell yosys to favor size vs speed?

It is arguable if the 2 LC solution is faster then the 1 LC, but for sure is bigger.
